title: Red-Black Tree Validity Check
description:
A red-black tree satisfies:
1. Root is Black
2. No two consecutive Red nodes on any path
3. All paths from root to Nil have the same number of Black nodes

Given a binary search tree in level-order where each value is prefixed by 'R' (red) or 'B' (black), and 'N' for null, determine if it is a valid red-black tree.
inputSpec: A single line: level-order encoding. Each non-null node is 'R:value' or 'B:value'. Null = 'N'.
outputSpec: YES if valid red-black tree, NO otherwise. If YES also print the black height.

botCodes.code:
vals=input().split()
n=len(vals)
left=lambda i:2*i+1
right=lambda i:2*i+2

def bh(i,par_red):
    if i>=n or vals[i]=='N':return 0
    is_red=vals[i].startswith('R')
    if par_red and is_red:return -1  # violation P4
    lh=bh(left(i),is_red)
    rh=bh(right(i),is_red)
    if lh<0 or rh<0 or lh!=rh:return -1
    return lh+(0 if is_red else 1)

if vals[0]=='N':print('YES 0')
elif vals[0].startswith('R'):print('NO')
else:
    h=bh(0,False)
    print('YES',h) if h>=0 else print('NO')
